title: Charred Norwegian Oil Flatbread
description: A romantic Norwegian dish featuring pillowy, charred flatbread made with oil, ideal for a low-glycemic-friendly dinner option.
instructions: Combine flour, salt, sugar, and yeast in a large bowl.
Mix in water and sesame oil until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover it with plastic wrap, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Punch down the dough and divide it into 2-3 equal pieces, depending on your preference for thickness.
Roll out each piece into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
Place the flatbreads in the air fryer basket, leaving about 1 inch of space between each bread.
Cook the flatbreads for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until they are charred and crispy.
Repeat with the remaining dough pieces.
Cut the flatbreads into wedges and serve hot.
